engineGuide.h2: BMW 7 Series Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the BMW 7 Series across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: BMW’s robust iron-block diesel for early 730d/740d. Famous for immense torque but known swirl flap and injector seal issues.
commonFailures: Intake swirl flap collapse into cylinders (~80,000 miles)
High-pressure fuel injector seal leakage
EGR valve clogging and excessive soot accumulation
entries.cta: Get quotes for M57D30 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Aluminium successor to M57. Delivered exceptional refinement but introduced rear timing chain and turbo vulnerabilities.
compatibleVariants: 730d, 730d xDrive, 730d M Sport, 740d, 740d xDrive, 740d M Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain guide wear and tensioner failure (~100,000 miles)
Turbocharger bearing wear and wastegate linkage rattle
DPF differential pressure sensor faults and regeneration failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for N57D30 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Current-generation modular diesel for G11 730d/740d. Highly efficient but susceptible to EGR cooler and oil pump issues.
compatibleVariants: 730d, 730d M Sport, 730d xDrive, 740d, 740d M Sport, 740d xDrive, 740d xDrive Luxury
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking leading to coolant loss (~90,000 miles)
Oil pump drive chain stretch and tensioner wear
AdBlue heater element failure and crystallisation
entries.cta: Get quotes for B57D30 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Petrol
entries.history: Flagship E65 745i V8 introducing Valvetronic. Notorious for early oil burning and valve stem seal degradation at high mileages.
commonFailures: Valve stem seal hardening causing blue smoke on startup
Plastic coolant expansion tank cracking and hose failure
Timing chain guide wear and tensioner rattle (~90,000 miles)
entries.cta: Get quotes for N62B44 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 4.8L Petrol
entries.history: 4.8L variant for the 750i delivering stronger torque. Shares V8 sealing vulnerabilities but features updated crankcase ventilation.
commonFailures: Valve stem seal failure requiring early intervention
Valve cover gasket oil leaks onto hot exhaust manifolds
Alternator bracket cracking and belt tensioner failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for N62B48 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Petrol
entries.history: Hot-V twin-turbo architecture for F01 750i. Compact design but suffered severe oil consumption and hot-start ignition failures.
compatibleVariants: 750i, 750i xDrive, 750i M Sport, 750Li, 750Li xDrive, 750Li M Sport
commonFailures: Piston ring and oil control ring clogging (~60,000 miles)
Turbocharger oil seal leaks and bearing wear
Hot restart stalling and fuel injector carbon buildup
entries.cta: Get quotes for N63B44 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated straight-six for E65 730i. Highly refined but known for cooling system leaks and Valvetronic actuator faults.
commonFailures: Electric water pump and thermostat housing failure (~80,000 miles)
Valvetronic eccentric shaft sensor degradation and misfires
Oil filter housing gasket leaks onto serpentine belt
entries.cta: Get quotes for N52B30 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Turbocharged successor to N55. Features closed-deck block and thermal management for improved reliability and daily usability.
compatibleVariants: 740i, 740i xDrive, 740i M Sport, 740Li, 740Li M Sport, 740Li xDrive
commonFailures: Plastic coolant pipe cracking under thermal cycling
Water pump impeller failure and thermostat housing leaks
Spark plug carbon fouling and ignition coil misfires
entries.cta: Get quotes for B58B30 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated V12 flagship for 760i/760Li. Complex direct injection delivers immense power but demands meticulous maintenance.
commonFailures: Ignition coil pack and spark plug failure causing misfires
High-pressure fuel pump seal leakage and rail pressure drops
VANOS solenoid clogging and timing chain stretch
entries.cta: Get quotes for N73B60 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 6.6L Petrol
entries.history: Twin-turbo V12 successor offering effortless torque. Retains complex ancillary sealing vulnerabilities and high replacement costs.
compatibleVariants: 760Li, 760Li xDrive, 760Li Individual, M760Li xDrive, M760Li xDrive Excellence
commonFailures: Turbocharger actuator and wastegate linkage failure
Fuel injector O-ring leaks and cylinder washout risk
Timing chain guide wear and tensioner rattle (~110,000 miles)
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B66 BMW 7 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol PHEV
entries.history: Combines turbo petrol with electric motor for 740e. High-voltage architecture introduces unique thermal and inverter failure points.
compatibleVariants: 740e xDrive, 745e xDrive, 745Le xDrive, 745e xDrive M Sport
commonFailures: High-voltage battery thermal management and cell degradation
Power inverter cooling pump failure and warning lights
Clutch actuator motor wear between electric and petrol modes
entries.cta: Get quotes for B48 Hybrid BMW 7 Series engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: BMW 7 Series Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The BMW 7 Series flagship saloon prioritises refined performance over absolute mechanical endurance, meaning long-term reliability heavily depends on how the N62, N63, and B57/N57 engine families are maintained. High-mileage examples frequently face catastrophic wear at critical junctions, prompting owners to weigh costly dealer diagnostics against specialist rebuild options. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components engineered to address these original failure points, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. The decision guide below breaks down realistic UK repair tiers so you can choose between targeted prevention or full BMW 7 Series engine replacement.

problems.group: BMW 7 Series N62 Valve Stem Seal Failure
problems.h4: BMW 7 Series N62 Valve Stem Seal Failure - Why Early V8 Engines Burn Oil
problems.rootCause: Viton rubber seals harden under sustained high cylinder head temperatures, allowing crankcase oil to seep past valve guides into combustion chambers during overnight parking.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Valve Cover Gasket & Seal Refresh
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of intake plumbing, new external gaskets, and partial top-end access.
repairOptions.longevity: Only masks external leaks. Does not resolve internal oil consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removed, old seals pressed out, new high-temp seals fitted without full rebuild.
repairOptions.longevity: Extends engine life 20-30k miles if block and bearings are sound.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full engine removal, new long block with upgraded seals, pistons, and bearings, bench tested and installed.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory compression and torque. Includes 12-month warranty.

problems.recommendation: If your BMW 7 Series N62 only exhibits mild smoke on cold starts, an intermediate seal replacement offers genuine value. However, once blue smoke appears continuously under acceleration or fault codes (P0171/P0174) trigger limp mode, the engine has already suffered cylinder washdown and bearing wear. At that stage, a full BMW 7 Series N62 reconditioned engine replacement is significantly more cost-effective than repeated partial repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N62 BMW 7 Series engine prices for your 745i or 750i

problems.group: BMW 7 Series N63 Hot Restart Issue
problems.h4: BMW 7 Series N63 Hot Restart Issue - The Twin-Turbo V8 Flaw
problems.rootCause: The V-valley layout traps exhaust heat directly against fuel rails and high-pressure injectors, causing petrol vapour lock and heat soak that prevents the starter from firing after short stops.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Fuel Line Routing & Heat Shield Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Insulated fuel lines, upgraded heat shields, and revised ECU mapping for hot starts.
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary relief. Does not address underlying piston ring or turbo wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Both turbos removed, rebuilt or replaced, VANOS filters cleaned, coolant system flushed.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost pressure and timing accuracy. Viable if compression is still >90%.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N63 Twin-Turbo Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete removal, new short block with improved piston rings, upgraded turbos and injectors, fully dyno-tested.
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates design flaws with modern components. 12-month unlimited mileage cover included.

problems.recommendation: The BMW 7 Series N63 hot restart issue is a documented design limitation that rarely resolves permanently through bolt-on heat shields. If your 750i or 750Li requires frequent jump-starts after motorway runs, fuel dilution has already contaminated the sump and degraded main bearings. Replacing with a rebuilt N63 unit pre-fitted with updated piston rings and fuel system components is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt N63 BMW 7 Series V8 replacement

problems.group: BMW 7 Series Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: BMW 7 Series Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - N57 & B57 Diesel Weakness
problems.affectedModels: BMW 7 Series 730d, 740d, 730d xDrive, 740d xDrive (2008-2022, N57D30 & B57D30)
problems.rootCause: Extended 15,000-mile service intervals degrade oil viscosity prematurely, starving the plastic timing chain guides and hydraulic tensioner, causing chain stretch and metal debris circulation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine front cover removed, new rails, tensioner, and guide fitted, oil pump primed.
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic jump. Only viable before rattle or misfire symptoms appear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removed, valves inspected, camshafts replaced, timing reset with upgraded OEM kit.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores valve timing if chain jumped but pistons avoided valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with reinforced timing assembly, updated tensioner, and fresh ancillaries.
repairOptions.longevity: Guaranteed timing integrity. Includes upgraded oil pump drive chain and 12-month warranty.

problems.recommendation: Listen for a metallic rattle on cold starts from your BMW 7 Series 730d or 740d. If caught early, a preventative BMW 7 Series timing chain kit replacement costs a fraction of a full rebuild and preserves your existing block. Once the chain has jumped or triggered limp mode, valve damage is almost certain. At that point, a fully reconditioned diesel engine with reinforced internal guides is the only safe path forward.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N57 and B57 diesel engine prices for your BMW 7 Series

problems.group: BMW 7 Series EGR Cooler Crack & DPF Blockage
problems.h4: BMW 7 Series EGR Cooler Crack & DPF Blockage - Modern Diesel Exhaust Failure
problems.rootCause: Thermal stress causes internal EGR cooler casting fractures, allowing coolant to leak into the exhaust stream. This contaminates the DPF substrate, triggering regeneration failure and excessive backpressure that damages turbo bearings.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Cooler & DPF Assembly Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Coolant system flushed, new EGR cooler fitted, DPF replaced, software updated for forced regeneration.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores exhaust flow and stops coolant loss. Viable if turbo and engine compression are healthy.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Reconditioned Diesel Long Block
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removed, turbo and injectors bench-tested, new long block fitted, full cooling and exhaust system calibration.
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates soot damage and restores torque curve. 12-month warranty on all internal and fitted components.

problems.recommendation: Coolant mixing with exhaust gases in a BMW 7 Series B57 diesel rapidly destroys the turbocharger and washes cylinder walls. If you are experiencing repeated limp mode, white exhaust smoke, or coolant warnings alongside DPF faults, do not attempt forced regenerations. Replacing the entire BMW 7 Series diesel engine with a fully tested, DPF-calibrated reconditioned unit is safer and often cheaper than piecemeal exhaust and turbo replacements.
problems.cta: Compare BMW 7 Series 730d/740d rebuilt diesel engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW 7 Series
finalCta.paragraph: When a flagship V8 or diesel powertrain shows signs of catastrophic wear, main dealer diagnostics and genuine OEM parts quickly push repair costs beyond the vehicle's residual value. EngineMarket cuts through the main dealer premium by connecting you directly with vetted UK BMW 7 Series specialists who stock upgraded, fully bench-tested replacement units. Every engine we quote for includes precision-machined components designed to outperform factory specifications, nationwide courier or mobile supply & fit options, and a comprehensive 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Compare transparent, specialist-backed BMW 7 Series engine replacement prices today and keep your luxury saloon on the road without the dealership price tag.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW 7 Series Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW 7 Series and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: BMW 7 Series Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement fitted to the UK BMW 7 Series is detailed below, complete with compatible engine codes, production years and known failure notes for accurate replacement matching.

items.title: BMW 7 Series 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre straight-six diesel has served as the commercial backbone of the 7 Series range across the E65, F01 and G11 generations, delivering a balance of refined torque and official BMW efficiency standards. Introduced with common-rail injection and progressively upgraded with sequential turbocharging, this displacement remains the most frequently replaced diesel unit in the UK marketplace due to high-mileage fleet usage and exhaust gas recirculation system wear.
commonFailurePoints: alongside EGR cooler cracking on later B57 iterations
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW 7 Series 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW 7 Series 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced in the modern G11 generation, the 2.0-litre four-cylinder turbo petrol replaced larger naturally aspirated units to meet stricter emissions targets while maintaining adequate power for the flagship saloon’s weight. This downsized architecture features direct injection and advanced thermal management, primarily powering the base 730i and serving as the combustion component in the 740e plug-in hybrid lineup.
commonFailurePoints: Plastic coolant pipe cracking under thermal stress
items.cta: Compare BMW 7 Series 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW 7 Series 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The inline-six 3.0-litre petrol has been a staple of the 7 Series since the E38 era, evolving from naturally aspirated smoothness in the N52 to the twin-scroll turbocharged efficiency of the B58 in modern generations. It occupies the middle tier of the petrol range, offering a seamless blend of daily refinement and accessible performance without the complexity of the larger V8s.
commonFailurePoints: high-pressure fuel pump degradation on turbo variants
items.cta: Compare BMW 7 Series 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW 7 Series 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: Serving as the primary V8 displacement for the F01 and G11 generations, the 4.4-litre twin-turbo N63 architecture delivers effortless acceleration but introduced complex hot-V packaging that concentrates heat between the cylinder banks. This configuration became widely recognised for its distinctive cooling challenges, making accurate engine code verification essential before sourcing replacement units for 745i and 750i variants.
commonFailurePoints: turbo oil seal leakage due to excessive V-valley temperatures
items.cta: Compare BMW 7 Series 4.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW 7 Series 4.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: Developed for the mid-life E65 refresh, the 4.8-litre naturally aspirated V8 provided a smoother power delivery and increased torque over the earlier 4.4-litre iteration without the complexity of forced induction. It remains a sought-after unit for enthusiasts prioritising linear throttle response, though high mileage often exposes age-related sealing vulnerabilities that require comprehensive reconditioning.
items.cta: Compare BMW 7 Series 4.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW 7 Series 6.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 6.0-litre V12 represented the pinnacle of pre-turbo flagship engineering, delivering ultra-smooth power through a dual overhead cam architecture with advanced variable valve timing for the 760Li saloon. Its sheer displacement and weight demand specialist handling during replacement, with most owners opting for fully reconditioned long-block units to restore the original grand touring character.
items.cta: Compare BMW 7 Series 6.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW 7 Series 6.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: Twin-turbocharging the 6.6-litre V12 allowed modern 7 Series flagships to meet stringent emissions standards while preserving effortless torque and silent cabin refinement. The N74 architecture features direct injection and advanced cooling systems, but its complexity and high compression ratios make it susceptible to specific turbo and fuel delivery failures that drive specialist replacement demand.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain guide stretch at high mileage
items.cta: Compare BMW 7 Series 6.6L petrol engine prices

groups.title: BMW 7 Series Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: BMW 7 Series 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Combining a turbocharged 2.0-litre petrol block with an integrated electric motor, the 740e and 745e plug-in hybrid variants deliver substantial combined torque while significantly reducing official CO2 emissions for fleet and private buyers. The combustion core shares architecture with standard B48 units but features reinforced internals and modified ancillary routing to accommodate the high-voltage powertrain integration. Not sure which engine size your BMW 7 Series needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK BMW 7 Series specialists.
commonFailurePoints: clutch actuator wear between drive modes

fuelTypes.h2: BMW 7 Series Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your BMW 7 Series engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early M57 diesel units to the latest B58 petrol and B48 plug-in hybrid powertrains.

items.title: BMW 7 Series Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre straight-six diesel has powered every 7 Series generation since the E65, dominating UK replacement enquiries due to high-mileage fleet use and exhaust system wear.
items.descriptor: The 3.0-litre straight-six diesel has powered every 7 Series generation since the E65, dominating UK replacement enquiries due to high-mileage fleet use and exhaust system wear.
families: M57D30 - robust iron-block unit (730d E65), swirl flap and injector seal vulnerabilities
N57D30 - aluminium successor (730d/740d F01), rear timing chain tensioner wear concerns
B57D30 - current modular diesel (730d/740d G11), EGR cooler cracking at higher mileage

Timing chain guide wear and tensioner failure beyond 90,000 miles

items.title: BMW 7 Series Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol 7 Series models span naturally aspirated straight-six and V8 architectures through to modern twin-turbo V12 units, with N62 valve seal and N63 hot restart issues driving significant replacement demand.
items.descriptor: Petrol 7 Series models span naturally aspirated straight-six and V8 architectures through to modern twin-turbo V12 units, with N62 valve seal and N63 hot restart issues driving significant replacement demand.
families: N62B44/N62B48 - early V8 (745i/750i E65), valve stem seal hardening and oil consumption
N63B44 - twin-turbo V8 (750i F01/G11), notorious hot restart and piston ring wear
N73B60/N74B66 - V12 flagship (760i/760Li), complex ignition and fuel system vulnerabilities

items.title: BMW 7 Series Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines

engineYears.h2: BMW 7 Series Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers every major engine change across the BMW 7 Series production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine code and compare rebuilt replacement prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The E38 defined modern 7 Series engineering, introducing refined straight-six and V8 powertrains that remain sought-after for classic luxury motoring today.
keyChanges: Launch of third-generation 7 Series with aluminium-intensive body construction
Introduction of M52 and M62 engine families with double-VANOS variable valve timing
Long-wheelbase E38 LWB variants introduced for enhanced rear passenger comfort
Final year saw transition to N62 V8 preparation for E65 successor
knownFor: Exceptional build quality and timeless Pininfarina-influenced styling
M62 V8 valve cover gasket and cooling system vulnerabilities at high mileage
M73 V12 complexity demands specialist maintenance and budget for replacement
Strong enthusiast following ensures steady parts and rebuilt engine availability
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-2001 BMW 7 Series replacement engines
years.ticker: E38 740i engine replacement • M62 timing chain guide wear • 750i V12 rebuild cost

years.preview: E65/E66 Generation: N62 V8 & N73 V12 Era
years.description: The controversial E65 introduced groundbreaking iDrive technology alongside the N62 Valvetronic V8 and N73 naturally aspirated V12, creating new failure patterns that drive today's replacement demand.
keyChanges: Radical Chris Bangle styling and first-generation iDrive infotainment system
N62 V8 introduced with Valvetronic variable valve lift, replacing M62
N73 V12 flagship engine debuted with direct injection and advanced VANOS
M57 diesel straight-six became the volume diesel option for 730d/740d
knownFor: N62 valve stem seal failure causing white/blue smoke on cold starts (~70k-110k miles)
N73 V12 ignition coil and high-pressure fuel pump vulnerabilities
M57 diesel swirl flap and injector seal issues at high mileage
E65 chassis electronics complexity can complicate engine swap commissioning
years.cta: Compare BMW 7 Series 2002-2008 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: N62 valve stem seal replacement cost • E65 760Li V12 engine for sale • 730d M57 timing chain failure
enquiries: N62 valve stem seal replacement cost

years.preview: F01/F02 Early Generation: N63 Twin-Turbo V8 Launch
years.description: The F01 generation introduced the controversial N63 hot-V twin-turbo V8 architecture, delivering immense torque but creating the infamous hot restart issue that dominates replacement enquiries today.
keyChanges: New CLAR platform with aluminium-intensive chassis and improved dynamics
N63 twin-turbo V8 replaced naturally aspirated N62, featuring hot-V exhaust layout
N74 twin-turbo V12 succeeded N73 for flagship 760Li models
N57 diesel replaced M57 with sequential turbocharging for 730d/740d
knownFor: N63 hot restart failure: engine cranks but won't fire after heat soak (~45k-90k miles)
N63 excessive oil consumption due to piston ring and valve seal design flaws
N74 V12 turbo actuator and fuel injector seal vulnerabilities
N57 diesel timing chain tensioner wear beyond 90,000 miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2012 BMW 7 Series V8 replacement engines
years.ticker: N63 hot restart fix cost • F01 750i engine replacement • 740d N57 timing chain failure

years.description: The Life Cycle Impulse update brought the revised N63TU engine with technical improvements, though many core design limitations remained for high-mileage owners.
keyChanges: N63TU technical update with revised piston rings, valve seals and cooling routing
Introduction of 8-speed ZF automatic transmission across the range
Enhanced iDrive system and driver assistance technologies
Final production years before G11 generation launch
knownFor: N63TU improved but not eliminated oil consumption and hot restart tendencies
N57 diesel EGR cooler cracking and DPF regeneration issues emerge
Higher specification LCI models command premium rebuilt engine pricing
Strong residual values make engine replacement economically viable for well-maintained examples
years.cta: Compare rebuilt N63TU BMW 7 Series engine prices
years.ticker: N63TU engine reliability • F01 LCI 740d diesel replacement • 750Li supply and fit cost

years.preview: G11/G12 Generation: Modular B57/B58 Engines Arrive
years.description: The G11 introduced BMW's modular engine strategy with the efficient B57 diesel and B58 petrol straight-six, alongside updated N63TU2 and N74 V12 options for flagship variants.
keyChanges: New CLAR platform with carbon-core chassis and significant weight reduction
B57 diesel and B58 petrol modular engines replaced N57 and N55 families
Introduction of 740e plug-in hybrid variant with B48-based PHEV system
Advanced driver assistance and semi-autonomous driving features debut
knownFor: B58 widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern turbo petrol engine
B57 diesel EGR cooler cracking and AdBlue system faults at 80k-120k miles
N63TU2 improved thermal management but retains some oil consumption characteristics
PHEV variants require specialist high-voltage certification for engine replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2019 BMW 7 Series replacement engines
years.ticker: G11 740i B58 engine replacement • 730d B57 EGR cooler failure • 740e hybrid engine availability

years.description: The final LCI update integrated 48-volt mild hybrid technology across petrol and diesel ranges, improving efficiency while maintaining the refined performance expected of the flagship saloon.
keyChanges: 48-volt mild hybrid system added to B57 and B58 engines for improved efficiency
Updated infotainment with larger touchscreen and enhanced connectivity
Revised exterior styling with larger kidney grille and LED lighting signature
Final production year before next-generation 7 Series launch
knownFor: MHEV systems add complexity but improve low-end torque and fuel economy
B58 and B57 continue strong reliability reputation with proper maintenance
N63TU3 and N74TU retain specialist rebuild requirements for V8/V12 variants
Low mileage examples increasingly sought for rebuilt engine programmes
years.cta: Compare latest BMW 7 Series 2020-2022 engine replacement prices

faq.h2: BMW 7 Series Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a BMW 7 Series engine in the UK?
items.answer: BMW 7 Series engine replacement costs typically range from £1,800 for rebuilt diesel units to £12,500 for V12 petrol engines, depending on engine code, variant and condition type. Supply-only pricing excludes fitting labour which adds £800-£1,800 nationwide through specialist workshops.
keyPoints: Rebuilt N57/B57 diesel engines average £2,200-£4,800 supply only
N62/N63 V8 petrol replacements range £3,500-£7,500 depending on generation
N73/N74 V12 flagship units cost £6,500-£12,500 for rebuilt specification
Supply and fit labour typically adds £800-£1,800 to engine price
Prices vary by engine code damage severity and supplier location
items.cta: Get tailored BMW 7 Series engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a BMW 7 Series?
items.answer: Replacing a BMW 7 Series engine is often worthwhile for well-maintained examples valued above £8,000, particularly E65/F01 V8 models where specialist rebuilt units restore reliability at a fraction of dealer replacement costs and extend vehicle life significantly.
keyPoints: Well-maintained 7 Series retain strong residuals supporting engine investment
Specialist rebuilt engines cost 60-70% less than main dealer replacements
N62/N63 V8 failures are design-related not maintenance neglect
Reconditioned units include upgraded components addressing original failure points
12-month unlimited mileage warranty protects your replacement investment
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMW 7 Series engine prices to assess your repair vs replace decision

items.question: What causes the BMW N63 hot restart issue on 750i and 750Li models?
items.answer: The BMW N63 hot restart issue stems from the V-valley layout trapping exhaust heat against fuel rails causing petrol vapour lock that prevents ignition after short stops, a documented design flaw affecting F01/F02 750i and 750Li variants from 2008-2015.
keyPoints: N63B44 engines 2008-2015 most affected by heat soak fuel vapour lock
Symptoms include cranking but no start after motorway runs or warm parking
Updated N63TU engines 2013+ feature revised cooling but retain some vulnerability
Permanent fix requires rebuilt engine with upgraded piston rings and fuel components
Temporary heat shield upgrades offer limited relief without addressing root cause
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt N63 BMW 7 Series V8 engines with upgraded components

items.question: Which BMW 7 Series engine is the most reliable to buy used?
items.answer: The B57 3.0-litre diesel and B58 3.0-litre petrol engines in G11/G12 7 Series models are widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern powertrains, offering strong longevity when properly maintained with full service history and correct oil specifications throughout ownership.
keyPoints: B57D30 diesel engines in 730d/740d G11 show excellent high-mileage durability
B58B30 petrol units in 740i feature closed-deck blocks and improved cooling
Avoid early N63 V8 engines 2008-2012 without documented technical update service
M57 diesel engines in E65 730d are robust but check for swirl flap issues
Always request compression test results and full maintenance records before purchase
items.cta: Compare prices for reliable B57 and B58 BMW 7 Series replacement engines

items.question: What are the symptoms of BMW N62 valve stem seal failure on 745i and 750i?
items.answer: BMW N62 valve stem seal failure on 745i and 750i models causes blue or white smoke on cold startup as hardened seals allow oil to seep into combustion chambers overnight, a common issue between 70,000-110,000 miles requiring prompt attention.
keyPoints: Blue smoke visible on cold morning starts that clears after warm-up
Increased oil consumption between services requiring top-up every 1,000 miles
Spark plug fouling and misfire codes P0300-P0306 may appear
Intermediate seal replacement costs £1,200-£1,800 if caught before cylinder damage
Full rebuilt N62 engine £4,200-£6,200 if bearings or pistons are affected
items.cta: Compare rebuilt N62 BMW 7 Series engine prices for your 745i or 750i

items.question: Should I buy a used or reconditioned BMW 7 Series engine?
items.answer: Reconditioned BMW 7 Series engines offer superior reliability and warranty protection compared to used units, making them the smarter choice for V8 and V12 replacements where failure costs are high and specialist expertise is essential for long-term peace of mind.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines include upgraded seals bearings and components addressing known failures
Used engines carry unknown mileage history and potential hidden wear issues
Reconditioned units typically include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard
Price difference narrows for V8/V12 where used stock is limited and expensive
Specialist reconditioners bench-test units before dispatch ensuring immediate reliability
items.cta: Compare reconditioned versus used BMW 7 Series engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: How long should a BMW 7 Series engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: With full service history and timely maintenance, BMW 7 Series diesel engines can exceed 150,000 miles while petrol V8 and V12 units typically reach 100,000-120,000 miles before major component wear requires attention or replacement depending on driving style.
keyPoints: B57/B58 modern engines achieve 150,000+ miles with annual oil changes
N57 diesel engines require timing chain inspection around 90,000 miles
N62/N63 V8 engines need valve seal monitoring from 70,000 miles onward
N73/N74 V12 units demand specialist servicing to reach 100,000-mile milestones
Full service records and correct oil specifications significantly extend engine lifespan
items.cta: Find BMW 7 Series engines with verified service history from UK specialists

items.question: Will a BMW 7 Series engine from a different generation fit my car?
items.answer: BMW 7 Series engines are not universally compatible across generations; E65 N62 engines will not fit F01 chassis without extensive modification, so always match engine code and chassis code when sourcing replacements for your specific variant to avoid costly compatibility issues.
keyPoints: E65/E66 N62 engines only fit 2002-2008 chassis with matching ECU coding
F01/F02 N63 engines require generation-specific wiring harness and DME programming
G11/G12 B57/B58 engines need compatible gearbox and hybrid system integration
Long wheelbase E66/F02/G12 engines generally swap with standard wheelbase equivalents
Always provide registration number to specialists for precise compatibility verification
items.cta: Get compatibility-verified BMW 7 Series engine quotes using your registration number

items.question: Does a replacement BMW 7 Series engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Every replacement BMW 7 Series engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with some reconditioned V8 and V12 units offering extended 24-month coverage for added peace of mind on premium replacements.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on all rebuilt engines
Reconditioned N62/N63/N73 units often include 24-month specialist warranty options
Warranty covers internal components gaskets seals and labour for replacement if failed
Keep all service records and use correct oil specifications to maintain warranty validity
Confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before completing purchase
items.cta: Compare warranty-covered BMW 7 Series engine replacement options from vetted UK specialists

items.question: How do I find the engine code on my BMW 7 Series?
items.answer: The engine code on your BMW 7 Series is stamped on the engine block near the oil filter housing or on the vehicle identification plate under the bonnet, and can also be verified instantly by entering your registration number online for precise replacement matching.
keyPoints: N62/N63 codes appear on block near oil filter housing on V8 engines
B57/B58 engine codes are laser-etched on timing cover or cylinder head
Vehicle identification plate under bonnet lists engine code alongside VIN number
Service book and V5C registration document often record the fitted engine code
Enter your registration above for instant engine code identification and compatible quotes
items.cta: Identify your BMW 7 Series engine code and get instant replacement quotes
